tasks.implementation_summary: Added `pending_merge` GitFinalizeOutcome/phase9_status support with PR metadata. Direct-mode protected default branch push failure now classifies branch protection, resets main to origin/default, pushes change branch, creates/reuses a PR, arms `gh pr merge --squash --auto`, re-reads PR state, returns `pending_merge` when auto-merge is armed, and collapses immediately merged PRs to shipped. Archive tool records pending_merge without completing release, archiving, closing issues, deleting branches, or cleanup. Added RED/GREEN coverage for protected push PR handoff, immediate PR merge collapse, sync archive pending_merge, and async callback pending_merge.

tasks.implementation_summary: Release gate completion now uses route-aware `resolveReleaseReachability` instead of local-only ancestry/push checks, so direct remote-backed release completion requires origin/default proof and PR pending states remain blocked. Release poisoned/completed-workflow recovery revalidates the same release proof before writing recovered gate state. `adv_change_archive phase9:"skip"` now validates main-checkout release evidence before archive/status/issue-closure and blocks missing origin/default or PR-merged proof. Existing bundle reconciliation now uses origin-aware evidence, including PR merge proof when phase9_status carries PR metadata.

subagent_reports.target: ADV archive/release-finalization remote-awareness audit (git-finalize.ts, gate.ts, change.ts, adv-archive.md, advance-workflow spec)

evidence.summary: verifyChangeBranchReachable runs `git log {defaultBranch}..change/{id}` against LOCAL default branch, not origin/{default}. This is the load-bearing reachability check; it returns reachable:true once the branch is merged into LOCAL main even if origin was never updated.
detectDefaultBranch resolves origin/HEAD first, then init.defaultBranch, then local main/trunk. Returns a NAME only; reachability/merge ops operate on the LOCAL ref of that name.

verifyDefaultBranchPushed IS origin-aware: fetches origin, compares local HEAD == origin/{default} ls-remote SHA. This is the only origin gate. If push was rejected by branch protection, this returns pushed:false.

finalizeRelease: after merge, pushToOrigin runs. status:'shipped' ONLY when push.status==='pushed'. Push failure/skip -> status:'blocked' with DEFAULT_BRANCH_PUSH_FAILED/SKIPPED. Runtime helper path does NOT report shipped on push failure.
Release gate enforcement: direct mode checks verifyChangeBranchReachable (LOCAL) then verifyDefaultBranchPushed (origin). The reachability half uses local default branch.
verifyReleaseEvidenceFromMain (existing-bundle retry, no worktree) checks verifyChangeBranchReachable (LOCAL) + verifyDefaultBranchPushed (origin) and returns status:'shipped' when both pass.
Terminal verb 'Merged locally.' fires when 'no remote configured OR push skipped OR push failed'. 'release ✓' footer appears for BOTH Shipped and Merged locally variants (L203).

rq-releaseFinalization01: explicitly authorizes local-only completion: 'If no remote exists or the push fails or is skipped, the archive may complete as a local-only result and must report Merged locally.' Spec sanctions the bug behavior.

findings: LOCAL-only reachability: verifyChangeBranchReachable (git-finalize.ts:390-409) compares LOCAL {default}..change/{id}. After ff-only merge into local main, reachable:true regardless of origin. gate.ts:1231 and change.ts:1294 both consume this.

Origin gate exists but is separate: verifyDefaultBranchPushed (git-finalize.ts:629-670) fetches origin and compares local HEAD==origin SHA. On branch-protection push rejection it returns pushed:false. The gate.ts/change.ts archive paths DO consume it (gate.ts:1243, change.ts:1313) and BLOCK release on failure.

Runtime helper blocks on push failure: finalizeRelease (git-finalize.ts:1062-1095) returns status:'blocked' (DEFAULT_BRANCH_PUSH_FAILED) when push fails; change.ts:4038 turns that into archive error 'Archive finalization blocked'. So phase9:'run' through finalizeRelease does NOT mark release complete on push failure.

Spec sanctions Merged-locally success: rq-releaseFinalization01 (advance-workflow.md:408, scenario .4 L455-468) says push failure -> 'Merged locally.' as a LEGITIMATE completion, and 'release ✓' footer is emitted for that variant (adv-archive.md:203). This is the contradiction at the root of the reported bug: the command-level terminal contract treats push-failed as a release-complete state.

No PR/auto-merge anywhere: grep for `gh pr`, `--auto`, `mergeStateStatus`, `auto_merge`, `gh api`, `pull request` across plugin/src returns ZERO hits in finalization code. PR mode (detectArchiveMode 'pr') only PUSHES change/{id} (pushChangeBranch git-finalize.ts:555) for handoff; it never opens a PR. The proposal's assumption that any auto-merge path exists is REFUTED.

phase9:'skip' bypasses ALL finalization: change.ts:3859 guards `phase9 !== 'skip'`. With skip, finalizeRelease/verifyReleaseEvidenceFromMain/release-gate completion/durable proof are all skipped; archive jumps straight to status transition (L4122). The release gate is NOT auto-completed by skip, but skip removes the runtime reachability/push enforcement entirely.

recoveryMode:'poisoned_history' bypasses ONLY status transition, not reachability: change.ts:4142-4209 — the poisoned fallback fires inside the catch for store.changes.save (the final status flip), AFTER finalization+release-gate+durable-proof already passed (L3859-4115). It does NOT bypass verifyChangeBranchReachable or verifyDefaultBranchPushed. gate.ts poisoned recovery (completeGateViaRecovery L354) for 'release' runs evaluateGateReadiness but does NOT re-run the git reachability/push checks — those live in the pre-signal block (gate.ts:1225-1265) which is SKIPPED when the signal throws and recovery is entered. So gate.ts poisoned-history release recovery CAN complete release without the reachability/push gate. RISK.

getDefaultBranch in utils/git.ts (L46-97) differs from detectDefaultBranch in git-finalize.ts: utils/git.ts strategy order is origin/HEAD -> local branches -> init.defaultBranch; git-finalize.ts order is origin/HEAD -> init.defaultBranch -> local main/trunk. Two divergent default-branch resolvers. finalization uses git-finalize.ts's detectDefaultBranch exclusively.
hotspots: plugin/src/tools/archive-helpers/git-finalize.ts:390 verifyChangeBranchReachable — single function defining 'reachable'; making it origin-aware (git log origin/{default}..change/{id} after fetch) is the minimal structural fix.
plugin/src/tools/gate.ts:1225-1265 release-gate enforcement block — only runs on the happy signal path; bypassed when signal throws and poisoned/completed recovery is taken (L1289-1313).
plugin/src/tools/change.ts:1294 verifyReleaseEvidenceFromMain — second consumer of local reachability; existing-bundle retry path.
docs/specs/advance-workflow.md:408 + scenario .4 (L455) — spec text that legitimizes 'Merged locally. release ✓' on push failure; must change if push-to-trunk becomes mandatory for release-complete.
.opencode/command/adv-archive.md:177,203,370 — terminal-verb contract emitting 'release ✓' for the Merged-locally (push-failed) variant.
risks: gate.ts poisoned-history release recovery (completeGateViaRecovery) skips the verifyChangeBranchReachable/verifyDefaultBranchPushed checks entirely — a remote-aware fix placed only in the pre-signal block (gate.ts:1231-1254) would still be bypassable via recovery. Fix must also gate the recovery path or move the check into evaluateGateReadiness.

Making verifyChangeBranchReachable origin-aware requires a fetch; offline/no-remote repos must still legitimately complete as local-only. Distinguish 'no remote' (allowed local-only) from 'remote exists, push rejected' (must block release ✓).

Two default-branch resolvers (utils/git.ts getDefaultBranch vs git-finalize.ts detectDefaultBranch) with different precedence could disagree; ensure the origin-aware reachability uses the SAME resolved ref the push targets.

Tests that will break if guard becomes origin-aware / push-failure stops being release-complete: gate.release-enforcement.test.ts (mocks verifyChangeBranchReachable/verifyDefaultBranchPushed), change.archive-phase9.test.ts (mocks same, asserts call args L393-398), git-finalize.test.ts:167 (verifyChangeBranchReachable local-only semantics), handoff-footer-drift.test.ts:293-299 + adv-autonomy-quality-assets.test.ts:208,242 (assert 'Merged locally. release ✓' is valid terminal output).
Spec rq-releaseFinalization01.4 + .5 + docs + command markdown are coupled — a behavior change needs synchronized spec/command/test edits or schemas/asset-drift tests fail.
open_questions: Intended end-state: should push-failed-to-protected-trunk become a BLOCKED (not release-complete) terminal, or should it require PR-mode handoff? This determines whether the fix is (a) origin-aware reachability that blocks, or (b) auto-routing to PR mode when direct push is rejected.
For repos with NO origin remote, should local merge still yield release ✓? Current spec says yes (Merged locally). Confirm this stays allowed.

Should the existing verifyDefaultBranchPushed (already origin-aware, already blocks on push failure in the happy path) be considered sufficient once the recovery-path bypass (gate.ts poisoned release recovery) is closed? The bug may be primarily the recovery bypass + the markdown 'release ✓ on Merged locally' contract, NOT the local reachability check.
suggested_next_commands: Read plugin/src/temporal/gate-readiness.ts evaluateGateReadiness to see whether release reachability can be enforced structurally in the readiness evaluator (closes the recovery bypass).
Read plugin/src/tools/change.ts:1460 completeReleaseGateAfterFinalization to confirm it does not re-validate reachability and relies solely on finalization.status.
Read git-finalize.test.ts:560-600 verifyDefaultBranchPushed test + change.archive-phase9.test.ts push-failure cases to scope test churn for the fix.
follow_ups: Proposal claim 'resolveDefaultBranchRef in archive.ts' is INACCURATE: archive.ts has no such function and no merge-base/reachability logic; all Phase 9 git logic lives in plugin/src/tools/archive-helpers/git-finalize.ts.

Proposal claim 'git merge-base --is-ancestor' is INACCURATE: reachability uses `git log {default}..change/{id}` (count unmerged commits), not merge-base --is-ancestor. Verbatim: git-finalize.ts:396-399.

Proposal cite 'docs/specs/advance-workflow.md ~L408' is CORRECT — the file exists at that path AND a mirror exists at .adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json:384. Phase 9 paragraph is advance-workflow.md:408.
Proposal assumption of an existing PR/auto-merge path is REFUTED — none exists; PR mode is push-only handoff.

subagent_reports.topic: Validate hands-off Phase 9 archive-release via PR + native auto-merge squash on protected-default repos

sources.label: gh pr merge manual
sources.summary: --auto merges only after requirements met; --squash confirmed; --admin/--disable-auto documented.

sources.label: GitHub Docs: auto-merging a PR
sources.summary: Repo Allow auto-merge prereq; enable only on PRs that cannot merge immediately (needs protection).

sources.label: cli/cli #8792
sources.summary: auto-merge off or PR immediately mergeable -> gh does immediate merge, not arm.

sources.label: cli/cli #13398
sources.summary: Error 'Auto merge is not allowed for this repository' when allow_auto_merge off.

sources.label: REST rules-for-branch
sources.summary: GET /repos/{o}/{r}/rules/branches/{b} returns repo+org rules; classic /protection misses org rulesets.

sources.label: cli/cli #5792
sources.summary: pr create errors if PR exists; gate via gh pr list --head --state open --json url.

sources.label: Docs squash semantics
sources.summary: Squash makes a new base commit; head commits not ancestors; ancestry falsely reports not-merged.

sources.label: gh pr view #2306
sources.summary: Squash-merged PR: state=MERGED, mergedAt set, mergeCommit.oid = squash SHA.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: SOUND. Pattern (push branch; idempotent PR create/reuse; arm native auto-merge squash; gate pending-merge with change active; reconcile via re-drive on PR merge STATE; no daemon) matches intended GitHub usage and avoids both the squash-ancestry trap and a polling daemon. Two conditions must be enforced or it silently degrades: (A) auto-merge is armable ONLY when repo Allow auto-merge is ON and base has >=1 required check/review (classic OR ruleset) so PR is not immediately mergeable; else gh errors or silently immediate-merges (#8792), in which case the change IS merged but ADV wrongly sits pending. (B) push-rejection detection MUST use ruleset-aware rules-for-branch, not classic protection (org rulesets with empty bypass_actors are invisible to classic). Reconcile on PR merge STATE only, never commit ancestry.

blockers: B1 immediate-merge collapse: PR already mergeable -> --auto not armed, gh immediate-merges (#8792). Re-read PR state right after arming; if MERGED reconcile now, else ADV stalls pending on a merged change.

B2 not-armable: Allow auto-merge OFF or no required check -> --auto rejected (#13398) or immediate-merge. Precheck allow_auto_merge + rules-for-branch; if not armable, do NOT report release done; manual-merge terminal.

B3 wrong detection API: classic /branches/{b}/protection false-negatives on org-ruleset repos; use GET /repos/{o}/{r}/rules/branches/{default}; reconcile on PR merge STATE only (squash breaks ancestry).
validation.notes: PR-state-based reconciliation is correct and sourced. Caution (not pass) because the design must add: an after-arm PR-state re-read to catch immediate-merge collapse, an armability precheck (allow_auto_merge + rules-for-branch) with an explicit manual-merge fallback that never reports a false release-done, and ruleset-aware detection. With those three additions the design is pass-grade.

subagent_reports.recommendation: 1) Detection primary = gh api repos/{o}/{r}/rules/branches/{DEFAULT} (ruleset-aware, all levels) + gh api repos/{o}/{r} --jq '.allow_auto_merge,.default_branch'; keep reactive fallback (attempt push, classify GH008/protected branch/required status check, reset local default to origin/default, route to PR) as second line since bypass_actors may permit some pushes. 2) Idempotent: PR=$(gh pr list --head change/<id> --state open --json number,url --jq '.[0]'); create only if empty via non-interactive gh pr create --repo O/R -B <default> -H change/<id> --title.. --body.. (avoids multi-remote prompt hang). 3) Arm: gh pr merge <num> --squash --auto, then immediately gh pr view <num> --json state,mergedAt,mergeCommit,autoMergeRequest; MERGED->reconcile now; autoMergeRequest!=null->pending; neither->not armed->manual-merge terminal, no release done. 4) Reconcile: gh pr view change/<id> --json state,mergedAt,mergeCommit; state==MERGED (mergedAt non-null) is the ONLY merged signal; never git log origin/default..change/id. 5) Auth: require gh auth status healthy; classic repo+read:org; pass all flags non-interactive; exit 4 -> explicit error, not false release done.
follow_ups: Packet defect: TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION anchors absent; proceeded with prompt scope per protocol; did not infer identity anchors (change_id from CHANGE line).
Live-probe whether gh pr view --json autoMergeRequest reliably returns non-null after a successful --auto arm before locking it as the armed-state discriminator.
Confirm ADV target repos use a gh token allowed to enable auto-merge on self-created PRs (self-PR restriction noted is Actions-bot-token specific; a user gh auth token is usually fine, probe once).

blocking_findings.label: blocker
blocking_findings.what: `pr_pushed` finalization is accepted as release-gate-completable, allowing archive retirement without origin/default reachability or merged PR proof.
blocking_findings.why: AC1/AC3/AC4/AC5 require remote-backed release/archive completion only after post-fetch origin/default reachability or merged PR proof; pending PR handoff must remain active as `pending_merge` or blocked. `completeReleaseGateAfterFinalization` admits `pr_pushed`, and `archive_mode: "pr"` tests assert successful archive output for `pr_pushed`. Gate completion also treats a merely pushed PR branch as enough in `plugin/src/tools/gate.ts:224`.
blocking_findings.fix: Do not treat `pr_pushed` as release completion. Convert PR-mode handoff to `pending_merge`/blocked unless PR state is MERGED, update `getReleaseFinalizationBlocker`, and replace tests expecting `pr_pushed` success with pending/blocked proof assertions.

blocking_findings.label: blocker
blocking_findings.what: Normal Phase 9 run does not implement the no-remote local completion path promised by AC6/docs.
blocking_findings.why: `finalizeRelease` attempts `git push origin <defaultBranch>` after local merge before classifying route. If no `origin` exists, push fails/skips and falls through to a blocked direct outcome rather than completing with local proof/no_remote. AC6 requires `Merged locally.` only for no-remote local proof; docs say no-remote archives may complete locally.
blocking_findings.fix: Classify route before push after merge. If route is `no_remote`, verify local reachability and return `shipped` with route `no_remote`/local proof; if route has origin, require post-fetch origin/default or merged PR proof before release completion.

wisdom_candidates.content: Legacy `archive_mode: "pr"` / `pr_pushed` behavior can bypass newer release-proof contracts unless all release-gate and archive-status paths require origin/default or merged-PR proof structurally.

verification.evidence: Focused tests passed: 2 files, 68 tests. Passing suite confirms current tests still encode the blocker (`pr_pushed` success), so additional/red tests are required for AC1/AC3/AC6.

risks: Full orchestrator evidence was reviewed as passing, but current tests do not catch the pr_pushed release-completion bypass or missing no-remote normal-run success path.
No repo changes made; worktree remained clean.
required_main_agent_actions: Fix release proof gating so `pr_pushed` cannot complete release gate/archive status; require `pending_merge` or merged PR proof for PR paths.
Add red/green tests for `archive_mode: "pr"` and manual release-gate completion showing pushed branch alone is insufficient.
Add/adjust tests for no-origin normal Phase 9 run proving local merge returns final no_remote/Merged locally outcome.
Rerun `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/change.archive-phase9.test.ts src/tools/archive-helpers/git-finalize.test.ts src/tools/gate.release-enforcement.test.ts` and then smoke/full as needed.

verification.evidence: Path preflight OK for reviewed files. Source review confirmed completeReleaseGateAfterFinalization only accepts status:"shipped"; blocked and pending_merge finalization paths return before release gate/archive/status/cleanup/issue closure; legacy pr_pushed only remains in type/test fixture and is rejected before release completion; direct no-remote finalizeRelease returns status:"shipped", route:"no_remote", pushStatus:"skipped" after local merge proof; PR mode without origin blocks with PR_WORKFLOW_REQUIRES_ORIGIN; PR/remote-backed pending routes remain pending_merge or blocked. Targeted rerun passed: 3 test files, 122 tests, exit 0. Reviewed supplied latest verification: full PASS and smoke PASS.

Fixed dead pr_pushed production status variant and redundant casts in touched release-finalization code/tests. Historical .adv/archive stale evidence rejected as out-of-scope historical archive metadata; active specs/docs/commands clean. Full/smoke/targeted tests passed.

wisdom.content: When adding fields to public Zod-backed schemas, run `pnpm run schemas:generate` after any Prettier pass on generated JSON. Formatting `plugin/schemas/*.schema.json` manually can make `schemas:check` fail even when source schemas are correct.

Release-state checks should go through the same route-aware reachability proof (`classifyFinalizationRoute` + `resolveReleaseReachability`) in archive, gate completion, and recovery paths. Duplicating local ancestry + push checks creates bypasses for origin/default and PR states.

Adding a new ADV MCP tool requires more than createToolMap registration: update `ADV_TOOL_NAMES`, `cli-bridge-contract` frozen snapshot, `docs/cli-surface-matrix.md`, `FIELD_POLICIES` for strict-mode placeholders, and `utils/tool-title.ts`. Tool-registry tests enforce the display-title piece.

User approved full-fix scope incl. PR+auto-merge via question tool. Agreement firmed (SC1-5, AC1-8, C1-5, DONT1-3, OOS1-3); ChangeContract minted (24 items).

Independent validator (adv-researcher) verdict CAUTION/pass-grade; refinements B1 (immediate-merge collapse re-read), B2 (not-armable → explicit terminal, no false release-done), B3 (ruleset-aware detection via /rules/branches, reconcile on PR merge STATE not ancestry) incorporated. Finalization-route classification (no_remote/direct/pr_auto_merge/pr_manual/blocked), pending_merge gate state, origin-aware reachability, phase9:skip + recovery hardening, detector/re-drive, spec+command updates. File-level strategy + test strategy defined.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved prep plan (reply: "approve"). Six-task graph persisted with contract refs for SC1-SC5, AC1-AC8, C1-C5, DONT/OOS boundaries. Worktree: /home/jon/.local/share/opencode/worktree/bdf259aa162ae192af5b18899ccdc653b085528d/change/fixArchiveReleaseWithoutMerge.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User accepted delivered work (reply: "accept"). Acceptance summary showed READY verdict, 0 review blockers, contract matrix 24 rows / 0 failing, preview URL not_applicable, full and smoke verification passed. Proceeding inline to harden per acceptance contract.

items.text: A remote-backed change reaching `release ✓` / `archived` implies the change is present on `origin/<default>`; never marked shipped while origin lacks it.
On a remote-backed protected default branch, Phase 9 ships hands-off via PR + GitHub auto-merge; the release gate stays pending-merge until origin reachability is confirmed.
A failed or rejected push to a remote-backed default branch never reads as success; the operator sees an honest terminal (`Pending auto-merge.` or `Blocked.`) with a reason.
`phase9:"skip"` and poisoned-history recovery cannot record release-complete without true origin reachability or explicit audited override evidence.
A repeatable detector lists archived-but-unmerged `change/*` branches across a repo set and can re-drive each through the PR + auto-merge path idempotently.
When release reachability is evaluated for a repo with a remote, it validates reachability from `origin/<default>` post-fetch, not local-only.
A simulated protected-main push rejection during Phase 9 yields no `release ✓` and no `archived` status; the change stays active with an explicit pending or blocked reason.
On a remote-backed protected default branch, Phase 9 opens or reuses a PR and arms GitHub auto-merge, and the release gate is left in a distinct pending-merge state that does not complete release.
`phase9:"skip"` cannot transition a remote-backed change to `archived` or release-complete without origin reachability or explicit audited override evidence.
`completeGateViaRecovery` for the release gate re-validates origin reachability and push, or refuses, instead of skipping git checks.
`Merged locally.` is emitted only when no remote is configured; with a remote, a non-shipped outcome yields `Pending auto-merge.` or `Blocked.` with a reason.

A detector lists archived-but-unmerged `change/*` branches (branch on origin, not reachable from `origin/<default>`); re-drive opens or reuses a PR and arms auto-merge with no duplicate PR and no force-push.

Spec (`docs/specs/advance-workflow.md` + `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json`) and `.opencode/command/adv-archive.md` updated so they no longer sanction `release ✓` on remote-backed push failure; the pending-merge terminal is documented; asset/consistency tests green.
Never force-push a protected or remote branch; PR re-drive must be idempotent (reuse an existing PR rather than duplicating).
Phase 9 must not leave local `<default>` merged-but-unpushed as de-facto source of truth after a failed push; reconcile to the PR path.
Preserve the workflow-bundle boundary (no `storage/` or `node:*` imports reachable from the temporal bundle) and cache-refresh discipline (`fireSignalAndRefresh`).

`gh` interactions must degrade gracefully; when `gh` is unavailable or the repo lacks `allow_auto_merge`, fall back to `Blocked.` (remote) or `Merged locally.` (no remote) with an explicit reason, never silent success.
Run test suites through the `bin/oc-test` throttle.
Do not land this session's already-stranded branches as part of this change.
Do not build a long-running polling daemon for merge reconciliation; rely on GitHub native auto-merge plus the detector/re-drive.
Do not modify the pokeedge/web repo-side Conventional Commit Check here.
pokeedge/web repo-side Conventional Commit Check exemption (separate repo change).
Landing the existing stranded branches (parallelizeWebUnitTests, hardenIntegrationE2eSuite, integrateListingEndpoints, addCnPricechartingIdentity plus stale set).
Operational redeploy of ADV to consuming repos (operational action, not code in this change).

documents.proposal:
# Proposal: Make ADV archive "release ✓" mean "actually merged to origin/main"

## Why
"Archived" must imply "shipped." On ruleset-protected repos, ADV Phase 9 does a local merge + direct push; when the push is rejected by branch protection it completes as **"Merged locally · release ✓"** while `origin/main` never receives the change — so it never deploys. The reachability guard only checks **local** main, so it passes. Discovered when a pokeedge-web agent archived `parallelizeWebUnitTests` that never landed; a cross-repo audit found ~10 unmerged change branches. Full code-level evidence is in `design.md`.

## User Outcomes
- When ADV reports a change archived / `release ✓`, the change is actually present on the branch that deploys (`origin/<default>`) — never silently stranded on a change branch.
- On protected-main repos, archiving opens (or reuses) a PR and arms GitHub auto-merge; the release gate stays **pending-merge** until the merge truly lands.
- A failed or rejected push never reads as success — the operator sees an honest terminal state (`Pending auto-merge.` / `Blocked.`) with an explicit reason.
- `Merged locally.` only ever appears when there is genuinely no remote.
- Operators can detect and re-drive already-stranded "archived-but-unmerged" changes across a repo set.

## Scope

### In Scope
- `plugin/src/archive/archive.ts` — `resolveDefaultBranchRef` (local-first ref resolution) + ff-only/reachability preflight (`merge-base --is-ancestor`); make remote-aware so the guard validates `origin/<default>` for repos with a remote.
- Phase 9 finalization path (`docs/specs/advance-workflow.md` §Phase 9, ~L408) — route local-merge vs PR + GitHub auto-merge by remote-protection / push-rejection detection.
- `.opencode/command/adv-archive.md` terminal-state instructions (L172-204) — add a `Pending auto-merge.` terminal; restrict `Merged locally.` to the genuinely no-remote case.
- Release-gate completion + `rq-releaseFinalization01` enforcement (gate/archive signal path) — validate `origin/<default>` reachability (post-fetch) for remote-backed repos.
- `recoveryMode` / `phase9: skip` bypass audit — neither may record release-complete without true origin reachability or explicit audited override evidence.
- New archived-but-unmerged detector / re-drive utility for cross-repo remediation.
- Regression test for a simulated protected-main push rejection during Phase 9.

### Out of Scope
- Landing this session's already-stranded branches (handled directly via PR + auto-merge, outside this change).
- pokeedge/web repo-side Conventional Commit Check exemption for ADV `Archive`/merge commits (separate change in those repos; squash-merge of the release PR sidesteps it and is the recommended pairing).

### Must Not
- Must NOT report `release ✓` when a push to a remote-backed default branch was rejected or skipped.
- Must NOT force-push or otherwise bypass a protected branch.
- Must NOT leave local `main` diverged-and-unpushed as the de-facto source of truth after a failed push.
- Must NOT allow `recoveryMode: poisoned_history` / `phase9: skip` to silently mark release complete without origin reachability.
- Must NOT destructively fail the archive on push rejection — preserve the pushed branch + already-written bundle and route to the PR path.

## Success Criteria
- **SC1** Archiving a change whose default branch is remote-backed + protected results in either the change present on `origin/<default>`, OR an open PR with GitHub auto-merge armed and the release gate left **pending-merge** — never `release ✓` while `origin/<default>` lacks the change.
- **SC2** The release reachability guard validates `origin/<default>` (post-fetch) for repos with a remote; `Merged locally` is reachable ONLY when no remote is configured.
- **SC3** A failed/rejected push to a remote-backed default branch never yields a release-complete terminal; it yields `Pending auto-merge.` (or `Blocked.`) with an explicit reason.
- **SC4** `recoveryMode: poisoned_history` / `phase9: skip` cannot record release-complete without true origin reachability (or explicit, audited override evidence).
- **SC5** A repeatable detector lists archived-but-unmerged change branches across a repo set and can re-drive each through the PR + auto-merge path.
- **SC6** Regression test: simulated protected-main push rejection during Phase 9 asserts the change does NOT reach `release ✓` and an auto-merge PR path is taken.

## Error handling / rollback
- **Push rejected (protection):** do not fail the archive destructively — leave the change branch pushed, open/reuse PR, arm auto-merge, mark release pending-merge; archive bundle already written stays on the branch (squash-merged later). No partial spec-delta application to origin.
- **PR/auto-merge unavailable** (repo lacks `allow_auto_merge`, or no remote): fall back to `Blocked.` (remote-backed) or `Merged locally.` (no remote) with explicit reason — never silent success.
- **Idempotency / re-drive:** detector + re-drive must be safe to re-run; reuse an existing PR rather than duplicating; never force-push a protected branch.
- **Local merge already done, push failed:** must not leave local main diverged-and-unpushed as the "source of truth"; reconcile to the PR-based path.

## Acceptance
Meets SC1–SC6, with the regression test (SC6) and a green re-drive of the blast-radius branches (SC5) as the primary evidence.
documents.problemStatement:
# Problem: ADV archive reports "released" without landing code on main

## Symptom (user-reported, reproduced)
An ADV agent in pokeedge-web archived `parallelizeWebUnitTests`:
- Release gate: ✓
- Phase 9: "done"
- Branch: pushed (`origin/change/parallelizeWebUnitTests`)
- **But: NOT merged to main.** `origin/main` does not contain the branch; the `Archive … apply spec deltas and bundle` commit sits only on the change branch.

Consequence: the change is marked **archived/released in ADV state**, but the code **never reaches main and will never deploy** (deploy pipeline only runs from main → staging → prod). A "shipped" change is silently un-shipped.

## Blast radius (cross-repo audit, this session)
Unmerged `change/*` branches that are up-to-date with main (behind 0) yet not landed — i.e. archived-but-not-shipped:
- pokeedge-web: `change/parallelizeWebUnitTests` (ahead 11 / behind 0), `change/hardenIntegrationE2eSuite` (#98).
- pokeedge: `change/integrateListingEndpoints`, `change/addCnPricechartingIdentity`.
Plus ~6 older stale branches (hundreds–thousands behind) that may be abandoned.

## Root-cause hypotheses (to confirm in discovery)
1. **Phase 9 direct-push-merge fails silently on protected main.** pokeedge/web require `Sharperflow CI Gate` via org ruleset; a direct push to main is rejected, so Phase 9 falls back to push-only but **still records release complete**.
2. **The trunk-reachability guard exists but was bypassed.** In a security-gates session this session, `adv_gate_complete release` BLOCKED with `RELEASE_REQUIRES_TRUNK_MERGE: not reachable from main` (rq-releaseFinalization01). So current ADV *does* guard this. The pokeedge-web agent got release ✓ anyway → either (a) pokeedge-web runs an **older deployed ADV** without the guard, or (b) the agent used `recoveryMode: poisoned_history` / `phase9: skip` to **force past** the guard.
3. **Even with a PR, the merge can be blocked** by pokeedge's Conventional Commit Check, which rejects ADV's own `Archive …`/`Merge branch …` commits — so ADV release PRs can't auto-land on pokeedge (separate but compounding; repo-side fix).

## Desired end-state
- ADV archive/Phase 9 must **not** report `release ✓` unless the change branch is actually reachable from main (trunk merge truly happened) — on **every** repo, protected or not.
- On protected-main repos, Phase 9 should **open a PR and arm GitHub auto-merge** (hands-off auto-merge + auto-update-branch are now enabled org-wide) rather than relying on direct push, and only complete release after the merge lands.
- If `recoveryMode`/`phase9: skip` can bypass the reachability guard, that path must require explicit, audited evidence and must not be reachable as a silent default.
- Surface a remediation/repair path for already-poisoned changes: detect "archived-but-unmerged" branches and re-drive them to a real merge.
- Coordinate the repo-side Conventional Commit Check exemption (merge + `Archive`/`archive(...)` commits) so ADV release PRs can auto-merge.
documents.design:
# Design: Remote-aware Phase 9 — PR + GitHub auto-merge, honest release gate

Validator: adv-researcher → **CAUTION/pass-grade**; refinements B1–B3 incorporated below (sources cited in discovery report).

## Confirmed root cause (verified, file:line)
- Reachability/push logic lives in `plugin/src/tools/archive-helpers/git-finalize.ts` + `plugin/src/tools/gate.ts` (NOT `archive.ts`).
- Happy-path guard already exists (since 2026-05-21 `makeArchiveMergeTrunk`): `finalizeRelease` returns `blocked/DEFAULT_BRANCH_PUSH_FAILED` on push failure (git-finalize.ts:1062-1095); `gate.ts` release block (1225-1265) runs `verifyChangeBranchReachable` (LOCAL `default..change/id`) + `verifyDefaultBranchPushed` (origin SHA, fetches first).
- Leaks: (1) spec/command still instruct `Merged locally. · release ✓` on push failure; (2) `phase9:"skip"` (change.ts:3859 guard) skips finalization+gate then flips `archived`; (3) `verifyChangeBranchReachable` is local-only; (4) `completeGateViaRecovery` (gate.ts:354) skips git checks on the signal-throw path; (5) NO PR/auto-merge path exists — "PR mode" is only branch-push handoff.
- Likely incident cause: deployed-version drift on pokeedge/web (pre-guard binary) + the wrong-terminal prose.

## Architecture: finalization classification

Phase 9 computes a **finalization route** from remote topology + protection, deciding BEFORE mutating local `<default>`:

| Route | Condition | Action | Terminal | release gate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `no_remote` | no `origin` remote | local merge | `Merged locally.` | ✓ complete |
| `direct` | remote, default NOT protected against this actor | local merge + `push origin <default>` succeeds | `Shipped.` | ✓ complete |
| `pr_auto_merge` | remote + protected default (push would/did reject) AND auto-merge armable | push `change/<id>`, open/reuse PR, arm squash auto-merge | `Pending auto-merge.` | ⏸ pending-merge (NOT complete) |
| `pr_manual` | remote + protected default, auto-merge NOT armable (allow_auto_merge OFF, no required check, or `gh` unavailable) | push `change/<id>`, open/reuse PR if possible | `Blocked.` (explicit reason) | ⏸ not complete |
| `blocked` | remote ahead / merge conflict | existing `DEFAULT_BRANCH_REMOTE_DIVERGED` / `MERGE_CONFLICT` | `Blocked.` | not complete |

### Protection detection (B3, ruleset-aware)
Primary: `gh api repos/{o}/{r}/rules/branches/{default}` (returns BOTH org-ruleset + classic rules; classic `/branches/{b}/protection` false-negatives on org rulesets) + `gh api repos/{o}/{r} --jq .allow_auto_merge`.
Fallback (belt-and-suspenders): reactive — attempt local merge + push; on rejection (`GH008` / `protected branch` / `required status check`), **reset local `<default>` to `origin/<default>`** (`git fetch` + `git reset --hard origin/<default>`, satisfies C2) and route to `pr_auto_merge`/`pr_manual`. The local merge is reversible because `verifyRemoteNotAhead` already guaranteed origin not ahead.

### PR mechanics (idempotent, non-interactive)
1. Detect existing: `gh pr list --head change/<id> --state open --json number,url` → reuse if present (C1 idempotency; `gh pr create` exits 1 if PR exists).
2. Create if absent: `gh pr create --repo {o}/{r} -B <default> -H change/<id> --title "<conventional squash title>" --body "<archive summary>"` (explicit flags → no interactive base-repo prompt hang).
3. Arm: `gh pr merge <num> --squash --auto`. Squash → single conventional commit (sidesteps Conventional Commit Check; pairs with OOS1).
4. **B1 re-read after arming**: `gh pr view <num> --json state,mergedAt,mergeCommit,autoMergeRequest`:
   - `state==MERGED` → immediate-merge collapse → reconcile to release-complete NOW.
   - `autoMergeRequest != null` → `pr_auto_merge` pending-merge.
   - neither → `pr_manual` (`Blocked.`), never false release-done (B2).

## Pending-merge state model
`finalizeRelease` gains outcome `status: "pending_merge"` (alongside `shipped` / `blocked`) carrying `prNumber`, `prUrl`, `autoMergeArmed`. `change.ts`:
- Maps `pending_merge` → do NOT complete release gate, do NOT flip `archived`; keep change `active`; persist pending-merge metadata (PR ref) on the change for the detector.
- Release gate left `pending` with a structured `stuck_reason: RELEASE_PENDING_MERGE` (informational, not an error).
- Archive bundle is already committed on the change branch (so it rides into the squash merge).

## Origin-aware reachability (AC1, B3)
- `direct` route: origin reachability = post-fetch commit ancestry on `origin/<default>` (extend `verifyDefaultBranchPushed` semantics).
- `pr_*` route: origin reachability = **PR merge STATE** (`gh pr view --json state,mergedAt` == MERGED), NEVER `git log origin/default..change/id` (squash breaks ancestry — B3 confirmed trap).
- Shared helper `resolveReleaseReachability(route)` used by BOTH the pre-signal gate path and recovery.

## Bypass hardening
- **AC4 `phase9:"skip"`**: before flipping `archived` for a remote-backed change, require release-gate `done` with durable origin-reachability proof; otherwise refuse with explicit error (skip stays valid for `no_remote`, already-`shipped`, or explicit audited override `recoveryEvidence`).
- **AC5 recovery**: `completeGateViaRecovery(release)` calls `resolveReleaseReachability` and refuses (does not record `done`) when origin reachability is unproven. Move the release git checks into the shared helper so the recovery path cannot skip them.

## Detector + re-drive (AC7, SC5)
New utility `archive-helpers/unmerged-detector.ts` (+ thin tool surface):
- **Detect**: for each `change/*` branch on origin, classify "archived-but-unmerged" = ADV state archived/release-pending AND not present on `origin/<default>` (PR state != MERGED and not ancestor). 
- **Re-drive**: idempotently open/reuse PR + arm auto-merge (reuse PR mechanics above); on `state==MERGED`, complete the release gate + finalize archived status. Safe to re-run; never force-push (C1).

## Spec + command updates (AC8)
- `docs/specs/advance-workflow.md` §rq-releaseFinalization01 + `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json`: add scenarios for remote-protected → PR/auto-merge → pending-merge; restrict `Merged locally.` to `no_remote`; new `rq-releaseFinalization01.9` (remote-aware honesty) + `.10` (pending-merge terminal). Run `pnpm run schemas:check` parity if schema touched (spec.json is data, not Zod — asset tests cover it).
- `.opencode/command/adv-archive.md` L150-210: replace "push failed → Merged locally. release ✓" with the route table; add `Pending auto-merge.` terminal; `Merged locally.` only for `no_remote`.

## File-level implementation strategy (LBP)
1. `git-finalize.ts`: add ruleset-aware `detectDefaultBranchProtection`, `classifyFinalizationRoute`, gh helpers (`findOpenPr`, `ensurePr`, `armAutoMerge`, `readPrMergeState`), `pending_merge` outcome in `finalizeRelease`, origin-aware `resolveReleaseReachability`, local-merge rollback on reactive reject.
2. `gate.ts`: extract shared release-reachability helper; use it in pre-signal block AND `completeGateViaRecovery`; add `RELEASE_PENDING_MERGE` handling.
3. `change.ts`: map `pending_merge` (no archived flip, keep active, persist PR meta); harden `phase9:"skip"` archived-flip guard.
4. `archive-helpers/unmerged-detector.ts` + tool registration for detect/re-drive.
5. Spec + command + `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` docs.
6. Tests (below).

## Test strategy
- Unit: `classifyFinalizationRoute` (no_remote/direct/protected/not-armable) with mocked `gh`/git deps (`GitFinalizeDeps.runGit` injection pattern already exists).
- AC2 regression: simulated protected-push rejection → asserts NO `release ✓`, NO `archived`, change stays active, `Pending auto-merge.` (route `pr_auto_merge`) — primary evidence.
- AC3: mocked `gh pr create`+`merge --auto`+`view` → PR opened/armed, release pending.
- B1: `gh pr view` returns MERGED immediately after arm → reconcile-now path.
- B2: allow_auto_merge OFF → `pr_manual`/`Blocked.`, no release-done.
- AC4/AC5: `phase9:"skip"` + recovery cannot record release without reachability proof.
- AC6: terminal-verb matrix; update `handoff-footer-drift.test.ts` + `adv-autonomy-quality-assets.test.ts` (currently assert `Merged locally. release ✓` is valid — must change to remote-aware).
- AC7: detector classifies a squash-merged PR as MERGED (not via ancestry) and an unmerged one as stranded; re-drive idempotent (no duplicate PR).
- Existing tests to update: `gate.release-enforcement.test.ts`, `change.archive-phase9.test.ts`, `git-finalize.test.ts`.
- Run via `bin/oc-test` (C5).

## Open follow-ups (non-blocking)
- Live-probe `gh pr view --json autoMergeRequest` returns non-null after `--auto` arm before locking it as the armed-state discriminator (researcher ag-EaoBLRO0).
- Confirm target repos' `gh` token may enable auto-merge on self-created PRs (ag-YyxI1Qdc).
documents.agreement:
# Agreement: Make ADV archive "release ✓" mean "actually merged to origin/<default>"

## Objectives

Make "archived" structurally imply "present on the branch that deploys (`origin/<default>`)". On remote-backed protected repos, Phase 9 ships hands-off via PR + GitHub native auto-merge and holds the release gate pending-merge until origin actually contains the change. Close every path (`phase9:"skip"`, poisoned-history recovery, spec/command prose) that can record `release ✓` without true origin reachability. Provide a detector + re-drive for already-stranded "archived-but-unmerged" branches.

## Verified Current-State

Reachability/push logic lives in `plugin/src/tools/archive-helpers/git-finalize.ts` + `gate.ts` (NOT `archive.ts`). The happy path already blocks rejected pushes (`finalizeRelease` → `blocked/DEFAULT_BRANCH_PUSH_FAILED`; `gate.ts` release block runs `verifyChangeBranchReachable` local + `verifyDefaultBranchPushed` origin SHA). Real leaks: spec/command still instruct `Merged locally. · release ✓` on push failure; `phase9:"skip"` skips finalization+gate then flips `archived`; `verifyChangeBranchReachable` is local-only; `completeGateViaRecovery` skips git checks; no PR/auto-merge path exists. Likely incident cause: deployed-version drift on pokeedge/web (pre-2026-05-21 guard) plus the wrong-terminal prose.

## Success Criteria

- SC1: A remote-backed change reaching `release ✓` / `archived` implies the change is present on `origin/<default>`; never marked shipped while origin lacks it.
- SC2: On a remote-backed protected default branch, Phase 9 ships hands-off via PR + GitHub auto-merge; the release gate stays pending-merge until origin reachability is confirmed.
- SC3: A failed or rejected push to a remote-backed default branch never reads as success; the operator sees an honest terminal (`Pending auto-merge.` or `Blocked.`) with a reason.
- SC4: `phase9:"skip"` and poisoned-history recovery cannot record release-complete without true origin reachability or explicit audited override evidence.
- SC5: A repeatable detector lists archived-but-unmerged `change/*` branches across a repo set and can re-drive each through the PR + auto-merge path idempotently.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: When release reachability is evaluated for a repo with a remote, it validates reachability from `origin/<default>` post-fetch, not local-only.
- AC2: A simulated protected-main push rejection during Phase 9 yields no `release ✓` and no `archived` status; the change stays active with an explicit pending or blocked reason.
- AC3: On a remote-backed protected default branch, Phase 9 opens or reuses a PR and arms GitHub auto-merge, and the release gate is left in a distinct pending-merge state that does not complete release.
- AC4: `phase9:"skip"` cannot transition a remote-backed change to `archived` or release-complete without origin reachability or explicit audited override evidence.
- AC5: `completeGateViaRecovery` for the release gate re-validates origin reachability and push, or refuses, instead of skipping git checks.
- AC6: `Merged locally.` is emitted only when no remote is configured; with a remote, a non-shipped outcome yields `Pending auto-merge.` or `Blocked.` with a reason.
- AC7: A detector lists archived-but-unmerged `change/*` branches (branch on origin, not reachable from `origin/<default>`); re-drive opens or reuses a PR and arms auto-merge with no duplicate PR and no force-push.
- AC8: Spec (`docs/specs/advance-workflow.md` + `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json`) and `.opencode/command/adv-archive.md` updated so they no longer sanction `release ✓` on remote-backed push failure; the pending-merge terminal is documented; asset/consistency tests green.

## Constraints

- C1: Never force-push a protected or remote branch; PR re-drive must be idempotent (reuse an existing PR rather than duplicating).
- C2: Phase 9 must not leave local `<default>` merged-but-unpushed as de-facto source of truth after a failed push; reconcile to the PR path.
- C3: Preserve the workflow-bundle boundary (no `storage/` or `node:*` imports reachable from the temporal bundle) and cache-refresh discipline (`fireSignalAndRefresh`).
- C4: `gh` interactions must degrade gracefully; when `gh` is unavailable or the repo lacks `allow_auto_merge`, fall back to `Blocked.` (remote) or `Merged locally.` (no remote) with an explicit reason, never silent success.
- C5: Run test suites through the `bin/oc-test` throttle.

## Avoidances

- DONT1: Do not land this session's already-stranded branches as part of this change.
- DONT2: Do not build a long-running polling daemon for merge reconciliation; rely on GitHub native auto-merge plus the detector/re-drive.
- DONT3: Do not modify the pokeedge/web repo-side Conventional Commit Check here.

## Out of Scope

- OOS1: pokeedge/web repo-side Conventional Commit Check exemption (separate repo change).
- OOS2: Landing the existing stranded branches (parallelizeWebUnitTests, hardenIntegrationE2eSuite, integrateListingEndpoints, addCnPricechartingIdentity plus stale set).
- OOS3: Operational redeploy of ADV to consuming repos (operational action, not code in this change).
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary

## Outcome

Implemented remote-aware ADV archive finalization so remote-backed changes cannot reach `release ✓` / `archived` without post-fetch `origin/{default-branch}` reachability or merged PR proof. Protected/default-branch failures now route to PR auto-merge pending or blocked states; no-remote archives remain valid local-only completions.

## Verdict

APPROVED

## What Was Built

1. Origin-aware release routing and reachability primitives: no-remote, direct origin/default proof, PR auto-merge, PR manual/block, and route-aware release proof.
2. Phase 9 integration for protected push rejection: reset/reconcile main to `origin/{default-branch}`, push `change/{id}`, open/reuse PR, arm GitHub auto-merge, persist `pending_merge` without release/archive side effects.
3. Release gate, `phase9:"skip"`, and poisoned-history recovery hardening: only shipped/no-remote/merged-PR proof can complete release; legacy `pr_pushed` is rejected.
4. Archived-but-unmerged detector and `adv_archive_repair` scan/re-drive tool with PR reuse and no force-push.
5. Spec, command, ADV instruction, and terminal voice updates: `Merged locally.` is no-remote only; remote-backed non-shipped states are `Pending auto-merge.` or `Blocked.`.
6. Cross-cutting tests and fixtures covering origin proof, PR pending state, skip/recovery proof, repair tool, docs/assets, and full-suite regression.

## What Was Verified

- Verdict: READY after independent adv-reviewer re-review attempt 2; 0 blocking findings.
- Tests: PASS — targeted release-finalization suites (280 tests), `src/tools/change.test.ts` (54 tests), review-remediation targeted suite (122 tests), `bin/oc-test full`, and `bin/oc-test smoke`.
- Preview URL: not_applicable — backend/tooling/spec/docs change; no frontend or browser-visible output.
- Contract matrix: 24 required rows passed/respected/not_applicable; 0 failed/violated/unknown.

## Remaining Concerns

None for this change. Operational redeploy to consuming repos and landing existing stranded branches remain explicitly out of scope.
